assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
clk_div toggles with an average period corresponding to 3.5 input clock cycles
No glitches or X states appear on clk_div during observation
cases.goal: Validate the fractional division behavior and duty‑cycle correctness of clk_div over multiple periods.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Observes average frequency and duty cycle after reset stabilization.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Observe clk_div behavior after reset over an extended observation window.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe over at least 14 input clock cycles to capture two full division periods.

expected_properties: clk_div remains stable (no toggling) while reset is asserted
clk_div resumes correct toggling after reset release without spurious pulses
cases.goal: Check correct handling of reset release relative to clock edges.
notes: Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
stimulus_intent: Assert reset for a few cycles, then deassert on a rising edge of clk.
timing_assumptions: Hold reset for at least 2 clock cycles before release.
Release reset on a rising edge of clk.

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: clk_div does not enter X or undefined states when clk experiences a short glitch
Module does not latch the glitch as a valid clock edge
cases.goal: Validate robustness of the divider to brief clock glitches.
notes: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
stimulus_intent: Inject a short pulse (glitch) on clk shorter than half the nominal clock period.
timing_assumptions: Glitch duration is less than half of the nominal clk period.

unresolved_items: Golden interface described clock 'clk_div', but the RTL port is not a scalar input-like signal.
